# youtrack_nested_group (Resource)

YouTrack nested group resource. Manages a group and its direct membership via user logins and subgroup names.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "youtrack_nested_group" "devops" {
  name                              = "DevOps"
  description                       = "DevOps team"
  own_user_logins                   = ["alice", "bob"]
  sub_group_names                   = ["Platform", "SRE"]
  require_two_factor_authentication = true
  viewers                           = ["security-team"]
  updaters                          = ["platform-admins"]
  auto_join                         = true
  auto_join_domain                  = "example.com"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Group name.

### Optional

- `auto_join` (Boolean) Whether matching users are auto-joined to this group.
- `auto_join_domain` (String) Domain used for automatic joining. Typically used with auto_join.
- `description` (String) Group description.
- `own_user_logins` (Set of String) Direct users in the group, identified by login.
- `require_two_factor_authentication` (Boolean) Whether users in this group must use two-factor authentication.
- `sub_group_names` (Set of String) Direct subgroups of this group, identified by group name.
- `updaters` (Set of String) Users or groups that can update this group. Values are resolved by user login first, then group name.
- `viewers` (Set of String) Users or groups that can view this group. Values are resolved by user login first, then group name.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) YouTrack ID of the group (computed)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import youtrack_nested_group.example <GROUP_ID>
```
